sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Rare Network requests Cardano treasury funding for Cardano title sponsorship of Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026 in Las Vegas, including main stage branding, livestream integration, governance programming, workshops, startup showcases, media coverage, community onboarding, free access, booth space, travel/lodging support, and related fees.

Deliverables: Cardano title sponsorship for Rare Evo 2026 main stage and global livestream, Cardano title sponsorship for Rare Dev Gov Day 2026 on July 28, 2026 at ARIA Las Vegas, Free General Admission access for the Cardano community, Complimentary booth space for selected Cardano ecosystem startup projects, Five Cardano-focused main stage panelist positions, Breakout room and workshop space, Livestream distribution with Cardano ads, Tier 1 media coverage for Cardano ecosystem announcements, Travel and lodging support for 20 Cardano ecosystem contributors or volunteers, Return of 20% of all VIP ticket sales to the Cardano Treasury

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, stated as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that 20% of all VIP ticket sales, with current VIP ticket retail value of $900, will be returned to the Cardano Treasury.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Rare Network states it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal reports Rare Evo 2025 metrics including 2,500 annual attendees, 80 exhibitor booths, 85 speakers, 100+ ecosystem partners, 20K+ livestream views, 4M social media impressions, 40+ Cardano projects represented, and 50+ enterprise participants.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The proposal states Cardano Dev Gov Day 2025 was free and publicly accessible, welcomed approximately 500 attendees, and included workshops, panels, networking, technical education, governance discussions, and a Charles Hoskinson keynote.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal claims the existing Rare Network infrastructure, venue relationships, sponsor network, production capabilities, volunteer coordination, and industry partnerships significantly reduce execution risk compared with building a new initiative from scratch.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal states that 20% of all VIP ticket sales, with current VIP ticket retail value of $900, will be returned to the Cardano Treasury.
Independent evidence for: Rare Network states it has been active in the Cardano ecosystem since 2020 and has operated Rare Evo annually for five consecutive years.

Independent evidence for: The proposal claims the existing Rare Network infrastructure, venue relationships, sponsor network, production capabilities, volunteer coordination, and industry partnerships significantly reduce execution risk compared with building a new initiative from scratch.

Independent evidence for: The proposal says sponsorship allocation may change if the mainstage or livestream package is purchased before the vote passes, in which case Cardano would receive a sponsorship of equal impact or dollar value.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Rare Network requests ₳2,750,000, stated as $660,000 at a $0.24 ADA valuation, to fund two Cardano sponsorship packages for Rare Evo 2026 and Cardano Dev Gov Day 2026." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal states that 20% of all VIP ticket sales, with current VIP ticket retail value of $900, will be returned to the Cardano Treasury." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
